query: PostgreSQL real-time analytics performance 50,000 events per second TimescaleDB
answer: TimescaleDB can handle up to 50,000 events per second for real-time analytics, leveraging its hybrid row/columnar engine for efficient data processing. It scales to petabytes and reduces query latency. TimescaleDB eliminates the need for separate ETL pipelines.

results.title: Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres - DEV Community
results.snippet: # Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres. Timescale unveils innovations for scaling Postgres for real-time analytics. ## Scaling Postgres for Real-Time Analytics on Time-Series Data: The Challenge. Postgres is one of the most powerful and flexible databases available, but as real-time analytics workloads grow, traditional approaches start to fail. To date, we’ve made Postgres the best database for real-time analytics—eliminating the need for specialized infrastructure. Now, in 2025, we’re pushing even further—removing bottlenecks, reducing query latency, and proving that Postgres can scale to petabytes of data and trillions of metrics—all in a single instance. This week, we’re unveiling the next wave of innovations that make Postgres even more powerful for real-time analytics. This was one of the most requested features for TimescaleDB—and on Thursday, we’ll show you how it can supercharge your real-time analytics workflows. Just Postgres for real-time analytics at scale. By the end of the week, you’ll have a complete picture of how Timescale eliminates bottlenecks, scales to petabytes, and brings the speed of transactional databases to analytics workloads.

results.title: How to Implement Real-Time Analytics with TimescaleDB - OneUptime
results.snippet: Learn how to implement real-time analytics with TimescaleDB, covering hypertable setup, continuous aggregates, real-time data ingestion.

results.title: How TimescaleDB helped us scale analytics and reporting
results.snippet: We’ve seen up to 100% improvement in SELECT query performance when we simply changed the order of account\_id and device\_id in our multicolumn index. That’s when we started to see performance degradation — particularly for large customers querying 7+ day time ranges across tens of thousands of devices. By aggregating this data ahead of time, we can collapse millions of rows into a few hundred per interval and query dramatically smaller, narrower tables. What was particularly interesting for us was range-partitioning on the timestamp column, because our customers wanted longer data retention, up to one year, and storing one year of data in one large table would have destroyed query performance. **Columnstore performance for real-time analytics:** TimescaleDB’s hybrid row/columnar engine, Hypercore, enables fast scans and aggregations over large datasets. For many teams at Cloudflare, TimescaleDB strikes a phenomenal balance between the simplicity of storing your analytical data under the same roof as your configuration data, while also gaining much of the impressive performance of a specialized OLAP system.

results.title: Real-time Analytics in Postgres: Why It's Hard (and How to Solve It)
results.snippet: Timescale lets you handle both transactions and analytics in your existing PostgreSQL database, eliminating the need for ETL (extract-transform-load) pipelines
